definitions.text: Relief from work or activity by sleeping; sleep.
examples: I need to get a good rest tonight; I was up late last night.
The sun sets, and the workers go to their rest.

definitions.text: Any relief from exertion; a state of quiet and relaxation.
examples: We took a rest at the top of the hill to get our breath back.

definitions.text: Peace; freedom from worry, anxiety, annoyances; tranquility.
examples: It was nice to have a rest from the phone ringing when I unplugged it for a while.

definitions.text: To cease from action, motion, work, or performance of any kind; stop; desist; be without motion.
examples: My day's work is over; now I will rest.
I shall not rest until I have uncovered the truth.

definitions.text: To come to a pause or an end; end.
To be free from that which harasses or disturbs; be quiet or still; be undisturbed.

examples: She ate some of the food, but was not hungry enough to eat it all, so she put the rest in the refrigerator to finish later.

definitions.text: Those not included in a proposition or description; the remainder; others.
To continue to be, remain, be left in a certain way.
examples: You can rest assured that a sick child will say when it's again ready to eat, so it won't starve and doesn't need to be cajoled into eating.
